LAW AND MOTION 302, JUL-17-2023. HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS ZACHARIAH COSTA, KEVIN COCHRAN, KEVIN GIBBS, MICHAEL HILLEBRAND, AND RAUL GONZALES' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE 2ND AMENDED PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. RULING - ARGUED. UPON CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT, ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONERS' "MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE 2ND AMENDED PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION" IS DENIED.- SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THIS DATE FOR COMPLETE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU) ***AFTER HEARING, AN ORDER IS SIGNED, (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)

calendar.matters:
Motion And Notice Of Hearing To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-08-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Arthur Lazear, Esq. 510-735-6316 Lazear Mack LLP for Petitioner.
Shane Cahill, Esq. 415-397-2222 Long & Levitt for Respondent.

ARGUED. Mr. Lazear contests the tentative ruling. Mr. Cahill responds and submits on the papers. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER ZACHARIAH COSTA, KEVIN COCHRAN, KEVIN GIBBS, MICHAEL HILLEBRAND, RAUL GONZALES' Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)

***After the hearing, an order is signed.***

Revised Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File 2nd Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jul-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Plaintiffs/Petitioners ZACHARIAH COSTA, KEVIN COCHRAN, KEVIN GIBBS, MICHAEL HILLEBRAND, and RAUL GONZALES' Motion for Leave to File 2nd Amended Petition to Compel Arbitration.

Appearances (via Zoom):

ARTHUR W. LAZEAR, LAZEAR MACK, (510) 735-6316, for Plaintiffs.

SHANE M. CAHILL, LONG & LEVIT LLP, (415) 397-2222, for Defendants.

Ruling - ARGUED. At conclusion of argument, the court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Petitioners' "motion for leave to file 2nd amended petition to compel arbitration" is denied. The petition sought to be filed is self-contradictory. In one breath, it demands that "the Court appoint a neutral arbitrator"; in another, it demands a "jury trial." (Id. at 4:4, 4:14-16, 8:16.) No authority is cited for such schizophrenia. Guan v. Hu (2018) 19 Cal.App.5th 495 addressed alternative causes of action, not forums, and is depublished in any event.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)

***After hearing, an order is signed, (See scanned order for details)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File 2nd Amended Petition To Compel Arbitration; Memorandum In Support; And Declaration Of Arthur W.Lazear
Off Calendar May-31-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:
